body:
### Summary

Removes **MUI 6 + Emotion** entirely from both the editor and the standalone web app, replacing them with [Base UI](https://base-ui.com) primitives and one shared, shadcn-style component vocabulary — and, as issue #757 asks, unifies the editor UI with the homepage design so the whole product reads as one app.

- **Editor — `@tumaet/apollon`.** Its internal primitives (Button, IconButton, Select, Tooltip, TextField, Typography) are rebuilt on Base UI and styled with plain CSS through the public `--apollon-*` custom properties. The library stays **Tailwind-free and Preflight-free on purpose**, so it keeps embedding cleanly into any host (VS Code, non-Tailwind apps). All popovers, toolbars, controls, and the minimap move onto the new system. A new **public theming API** (`createApollonTheme()` plus `theme` / `dataTheme` props) lets hosts theme the editor without hand-writing CSS variables.
- **Web app — `@tumaet/webapp`.** Migrates to the new shared **`@tumaet/ui`** library. The editor shell, navbar, modals, version history, and the home gallery now share one accessible component set and one set of design tokens with the homepage — a cohesive floating-glass chrome where every action control (File, Help, Share, Favorites, Refine, Import, Theme, version, save) reads as one family: an icon plus a label that reveals as space allows, backed by a single instant tooltip.
- **`@tumaet/ui` (new internal package).** The shared design system — 28 components on Base UI + Tailwind v4 + `lucide-react` — with Storybook coverage for every component. Private (`0.0.0`), so it carries no changelog of its own; its user-visible impact ships through `@tumaet/webapp`.

Icons are unified on `lucide-react`; the custom SVG icon adapter is gone.

### What changed

| Area | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Editor primitives | MUI + Emotion | Base UI, styled via `--apollon-*` CSS |
| Web app components | MUI 6 / Emotion | `@tumaet/ui` (shadcn on Base UI + Tailwind) |
| Popovers · tooltips · select · dialogs · menus · sheets | MUI | Base UI (focus trap, `Escape`, ARIA out of the box) |
| Icons | custom SVG wrappers + `makeIcon()` | direct `lucide-react` |
| Design tokens | scattered | single source of truth, exposed to Tailwind via `@theme` |
| Editor + home chrome | two separate looks | one cohesive floating-glass language, light + dark |
| Editor theming | CSS variables only | `--apollon-*` contract **+** typed `createApollonTheme()` helper |
| Component docs | — | Storybook (one monorepo-wide host in the web app) |

### Beyond the migration — design-system, theming, and quality hardening

The branch doesn't just swap frameworks; it brings the migrated surfaces to A+:

- **Editor popovers — modernized and made cohesive.** Every node/edge/feedback popover was audited and unified onto one section/heading grammar: a "Style" heading for name-less nodes, the «stereotype» toggle moved to its own row (component/deployment), an **embed-safe `Checkbox` primitive** replacing the raw checkboxes (reachability/SFC), consistent "+ Add" affordances and bare-noun placeholders, and **multiline name fields that start single-line and grow per newline** (the 64px floor is gone).
- **Assessment feedback — redesigned.** The give/see-feedback popovers became a **Scorecard** — a tone-coded score pill (positive/negative/zero/ungraded) with matching feedback states — and the **on-canvas feedback badge was modernized to mirror it exactly** (a soft tone-tinted disc, not the old washed-out gray circle). All assessment colours flow through the token system (`--apollon-assessment-*`, light + dark deltas); they're **WCAG-AA and axe-gated**, no hardcoded hex.
- **Storybook — raised to A+.** The editor stories now cover **every diagram type** plus the give/see-feedback popovers, full-editor **assessment** (give + a graded review that shows the on-canvas badges), **highlight** (the interactive-element picker *and* the imperative `setElementHighlights` host API), **assessment selection**, and **modes** — all **test-gated in a real browser with a11y enforced**, and scannable in autodocs. `@tumaet/ui` and webapp stories are likewise hardened (real interaction tests, dark/matrix coverage, no theatre).
- **Naming & structure convergence.** Boolean-trap props → variant unions; mislabeled navbar components renamed (`FileMenu` / `HelpMenu` / `BrandLockup` / `EditorHeader`); container/presentational extractions; a typed modal registry (no more `Record<string, unknown>`).
- **Docs corrected + completed.** The UI/Overview, Theming, Tokens, and library theming/API docs were reconciled against the actual code; a `cssVariableContract` test enforces that the public token table stays in sync. The last MUI-ism — the fake `sx` prop on the editor's Typography/TextField wrappers — was removed.
- **Fixes.** Palette drag no longer jumps on grab and matches the drop size at any zoom; the theme switch is a single instant flip; several home/legal e2e tests fixed (including a real notch-clearance `calc()` bug) and the swimlane keyboard-reorder e2e hardened.

### Release note

The published changelog entries are the three changesets this PR adds, each written in its track's voice:

- **`@tumaet/apollon` (minor)** — Removes MUI and Emotion from the editor; controls, popovers, toolbars, and the minimap are rebuilt on lightweight Base UI primitives styled entirely through the public `--apollon-*` CSS variables — smaller dependency footprint, no Emotion runtime, no MUI style collisions, look and behaviour unchanged.
- **`@tumaet/apollon` (minor)** — Adds a public theming API (`createApollonTheme()` + `theme` / `dataTheme` props) so hosts can theme the editor with a typed helper instead of hand-writing CSS variables.
- **`@tumaet/webapp` (minor)** — Rebuilds the web app's interface on one shared component system, so the editor, dialogs, version history, and gallery share a consistent look matching the homepage in light and dark; menus, dialogs, and sheets are fully keyboard-accessible; the app loads lighter after dropping its old UI framework.

`@tumaet/server` rides a paired **minor** bump (Changesets `fixed` group with `@tumaet/webapp`); its image is republished but its behaviour is unchanged.

### Implementation notes

- **Why two styling modes (the accurate version).** The editor is published and embeds in arbitrary hosts, so it can't ship Tailwind/Preflight (utility-class and reset collisions). **Most `@tumaet/ui` components are standard shadcn** with inline Tailwind utilities and are **webapp-only** (the webapp runs Tailwind). The **five primitives the editor shares — `button`, `icon-button`, `input`, `textarea`, `tooltip` — are authored differently**: an empty `cva()` (variants only type/validate) plus `data-slot` rules in `src/styles/components.css`. `build:css` compiles **only that file** into a Preflight-free, Tailwind-free `dist/components.css` containing exactly those five primitives + the token values — no bare utility classes — which is what makes it embed-safe.
- **Token layering.** `--primitive-*` → `--apollon-*` (public editor API) → `--home-*` (web app) → `--color-*` (shadcn/Tailwind `@theme inline`). Light values in `:root`, dark deltas in `:root[data-theme="dark"]`; a single `data-theme` flip drives both, and a scoped `[data-theme="dark"]` themes a mounted editor without touching `:root`.
- **Accessibility.** Dialogs, menus, and sheets get focus trapping, `Escape` handling, and ARIA wiring from Base UI. Icon-only controls carry a real accessible name and an instant shared tooltip rather than a native `title`. New assessment colours are WCAG-AA and the editor story suite runs the axe color-contrast gate.
- **Verification surface.** A single Storybook host (in the web app) covers `@tumaet/ui`, the editor (organized by diagram type), and the web app; light/dark is reviewed there (stories and Docs). The editor's harness-based stories are test-gated.
- **Dead-code & dependency hygiene — [knip](https://knip.dev).** A migration this size churns a lot of modules, so the branch adds knip (`knip.json` plus a root `pnpm knip` script) to catch unused files, exports, and dependencies across every workspace. It is how the dead MUI-era components and stories were found and removed. It runs on demand and is **not** yet a blocking CI gate.

#### Scope & transparency

- This is a **large diff (~485 files)**, but a single cohesive change: the migration plus the editor↔homepage unification that issue #757 calls for, brought to a finished A+ standard. They land together because you can't take the home gallery and editor chrome off MUI without rebuilding those components — and rebuilding them is what makes the two surfaces match.
- The branch is **up to date with `main`** (0 commits behind). Features that already shipped on `main` — activity swimlanes, diagram embedding, the server-render fixes, and the original floating-glass editor chrome (#778) — are **not** part of this diff and keep their own changesets.
- `@tumaet/ui` is intentionally **private**, so it is excluded from Changesets tracking; everything a user or operator notices flows through the `@tumaet/webapp` entry above.

### Steps for testing

1. `pnpm install && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test`
2. **Editor** — open each diagram type: double-click nodes and edges (popovers), hover the toolbar (tooltips), use zoom / fit / undo / redo and the minimap. In Assessment mode, give feedback (the Scorecard) and review a graded diagram (the on-canvas badges).
3. **Web app** — exercise the navbar, New Diagram, Share, version history, and the home gallery; toggle light ↔ dark; resize from desktop down to a 390 px phone and confirm the action controls keep a stable order and reveal tooltips instantly.
4. **Storybook** — `pnpm --filter @tumaet/webapp run storybook` (→ http://localhost:6006), browse **Editor** (by diagram type, incl. the Assessment / Feedback / Modes stories) + **Webapp** + **UI**, and flip the theme toolbar on both stories and Docs.
5. **E2E** — `pnpm run test:e2e`.

### Screenshots / screencasts

technicalSummary: Migrates the Apollon editor and standalone web application away from MUI/Emotion toward Base UI and shadcn-style components, modernizing the visual system and unifying UI structure across the editor and web app.

body:
<!-- Thanks for contributing to Apollon! Before you submit your pull request, please make sure to check the following boxes by putting an x in the [ ] (don't: [x ], [ x], do: [x]) -->

### Checklist

- [ ] I linked PR with a related issue
- [x] I added multiple screenshots/screencasts of my UI changes

### Motivation and Context

Edges were the roughest part of the editor. Step edges bent unpredictably, manually placed waypoints were lost when an endpoint was reconnected, endpoint reconnection used bespoke logic that diverged from React Flow's own behaviour, and the half-circle "arc" connection handles could overlap each other on small nodes.

This PR makes edge routing, interaction, and connection handles behave predictably and look clean. It also keeps the collaborative document small: only the *inputs* to an edge (its endpoints, manually placed waypoints, and handles) are synced through Yjs — never the computed pixel geometry — so peers don't thrash shared state or drift apart.

### Description

**Edge bending & waypoints**
- Step-edge bending is consolidated and predictable: each draggable bend handle moves exactly one segment, snapped to the 5px grid, and the rest of the path stays put.
- An in-progress drag is now driven through React state, so the path, the dragged handle, the edit toolbar, and per-type decorations all follow the drag **live** instead of jumping only on release.
- Bends survive endpoint reconnection where the geometry allows, and clamp to the last valid shape (rather than snapping all the way back) when dragged past a limit.

**Endpoint reconnection**
- Adopts React Flow's native reconnect API in place of the previous custom flow, with a connection-line preview. The opposite endpoint stays anchored while you drag the other end.

**Connection handles (9-slot model)**
- Each side exposes a 9-slot offset model: five *named* handle IDs at the even slots (corner / mid-corner / middle / mid-corner / corner) render the visible arcs, and four *between* IDs at the odd slots stay addressable as hidden anchors so saved edges still resolve when a node is resized between layout stages.
- Arc draggers are laid out in symmetric stages by side length — **1** centred arc on short sides, **3** at corners + middle, up to **5** evenly spaced — so they never visually overlap, including when zoomed out. All legacy handle IDs remain addressable for backwards-compatible diagrams.
- Handles, endpoint hit targets, and the edit toolbar keep a predictable on-screen size across zoom; the canvas/handle snap grid moved from 10px → 5px so connection points stay grid-aligned.

**Decorations**
- The SFC transition bar + condition label sit exactly on the edge centre (standard notation) and follow the edge while it is dragged; decorative SVG is pointer-transparent so it can't steal a bend handle's grab.

**Sync & migration**
- Computed geometry is no longer persisted; only edge inputs are synced.
- `EdgeTransformer` strips stale runtime-only geometry from imported diagrams so older models open cleanly.

**Tests**
- ~1.5k lines of unit + Playwright E2E coverage for the bend FSM, orthogonal point preservation, the 9-slot/5px-grid handle contract, zoom-adaptive handles, endpoint markers, migration, and the decoration/reconnect behaviour.

> **Scope note (transparency):** an experimental worker-backed orthogonal *auto-routing* engine (A\* + visibility graph + spatial index) was prototyped earlier on this branch and then **removed** before merge — it added significant complexity for little benefit over React Flow's step routing plus manual waypoints. This PR ships the simpler, predictable approach described above; the routing engine is **not** part of it.

### Steps for Testing

1. Draw an edge between two nodes — verify it renders as a clean orthogonal step path.
2. Drag one of the nodes — verify the path updates smoothly without flicker.
3. Drag a bend handle on a step edge — verify only that segment moves, it snaps to the grid, and the edit toolbar/labels follow the drag live (not just on release).
4. Drag an edge endpoint to a handle on the same node, then to a different node — verify the opposite endpoint stays anchored and manual bends are preserved when geometry allows.
5. Drop a **Use Case** association/ellipse and drop/reconnect an edge near it — verify the edge always attaches to a visible handle and never disappears.
6. Open a diagram saved with an older Apollon version — verify edges render correctly with no manual fixes.
7. With two tabs collaborating via Yjs, move nodes in one tab — verify the other re-routes locally with no edge geometry thrashing through the shared doc.
8. Drop a small Class node and grow it — verify the side shows **1** centred arc, then **3** (corners + middle), then **5** evenly-spaced arcs, never overlapping at any size or zoom.
9. Add an SFC condition to a transition — verify the crossbar + label sit on the edge centre and follow it while bending.
10. Drag a node on the canvas — verify it snaps to 5px increments.
11. Run `pnpm test` in `library/` and `pnpm test:visual` + `pnpm test:e2e` in `standalone/webapp/` — all suites pass.

body:
<!-- Thanks for contributing to Apollon! Before you submit your pull request, please make sure to check the following boxes by putting an x in the [ ] (don't: [x ], [ x], do: [x]) -->

<!-- If your pull request is not ready for review yet, create a draft pull request! -->

### Checklist

- [x] I linked PR with a related issue
- [ ] I added multiple screenshots/screencasts of my UI changes

### Motivation and Context

This PR completes https://github.com/ls1intum/Apollon/issues/585

The webapp previously dropped users straight into a blank `ApollonLocal` editor on `/`, with no overview of saved work, no entry point for templates, and no way to deep-link a specific diagram. New users had nothing to anchor on; returning users had to dig through the "Load Diagram" modal to find anything. We needed (1) a real landing experience that surfaces saved and shared work, and (2) a routing model where every diagram has a stable, shareable URL.

This PR delivers those two things: **routing** and a **new home page**.

### Description

#### 1. Routing

Each diagram now lives at a stable URL, and `/` is a dedicated landing page instead of an editor mount.

- `/` → `HomePage`
- `/local/:id` → `ApollonLocal` (the local editor; previously mounted directly at `/`)
- `/shared/:id?view=<mode>` → `ApollonWithConnection` (shared/collaborative editor; was `/:diagramId`)
- `/playground`, `/imprint`, `/privacy` → unchanged pages
- `/:id?view=…` → `LegacySharedDiagramRedirect`: old single-segment shared links are 301-style redirected to `/shared/:id` (preserving the `?view=` query); anything without a `view` param falls through to `ErrorPage`.
- `*` → `ErrorPage`

Supporting pieces:
- The editor `Navbar` is only mounted off the home route; `HomePage` renders its own `HomeNavbar`.
- All page chunks are `lazy`-loaded, and the `Navbar`, `ToastContainer` (`DeferredToastContainer`), and modal wrapper are deferred for faster first paint behind a single `AppLoadingScreen` fallback.
- Shared-diagram URL handling is centralised in `utils/sharedDiagramLinks.ts` (`buildSharedDiagramPath` / `buildSharedDiagramUrl`, view normalization, the four share modes: Edit / Collaborate / Give feedback / Review feedback).
- `ApollonWithConnection` reads `:id` from the path, and on a failed initial load now shows a dedicated error screen (404 / server-unavailable / generic) with **Retry** and **Go home** instead of a toast + forced redirect.

#### 2. New home page — "Your diagrams" dashboard

A single, unified `DiagramGallery` dashboard replaces the blank editor landing.

- **Gallery** (`DiagramGallery`): a searchable, filterable list of diagrams with a "Your diagrams" heading and live count.
  - Source segmented control: **All / Local / Shared**.
  - Filters: search-by-name, diagram-type dropdown, sort (Alphabetical / Date created / Last viewed) + order, and a Favorites toggle.
  - **Grid and table** view modes via a segmented control.
  - Infinite scroll (`IntersectionObserver` sentinel, 9 per page) — no "Load more" button.
- **Cards** (`DiagramCard`): thumbnail preview, title, created/last-modified metadata, type + source/mode badges, a favorite star, and a three-dot actions menu (Open / Duplicate / Share / Delete-with-confirm; shared diagrams expose copy-link, change-mode, and remove).
- **Shared diagrams**: local browser storage of shared entries (`utils/sharedDiagramStorage.ts`), fetched and merged into the gallery; expired links render an "expired" card state. A new `ShareDashboardModal` creates a shareable link and lets the user switch the default view mode inline.
- **Thumbnails**: the editor generates a normalized SVG thumbnail after edits; the home page warms thumbnails for visible cards via `useDiagramThumbnailWarmup` (idle-scheduled, sequence-guarded, LRU-pruned theme cache, light/dark variants). Thumbnails are kept in zustand but excluded from `partialize` to avoid localStorage bloat.
- **Design system harmonization**: shared `HomeButton` and `HomeDialog*` primitives give every home button/dialog one source of truth for variants and hover/focus/disabled behaviour. New-diagram and template modals render a "home dialog" variant; a `ModalProgressContext` drives an in-modal progress bar. The post-restore Undo affordance and other notifications were unified onto `react-toastify` (the old MUI `SnackbarMessage` was removed).
- **Theming**: a full set of `--home-*` CSS variables (surfaces, text, borders, accent, tags, shadows) in `themings.json` + `webapp.css`, light and dark.

#### Tests

- New `tests/e2e/home-page.spec.ts` covering initial load, empty state, gallery search/filter/sort/favorites, source filter, grid↔table toggle, infinite scroll, and card actions (28 tests).
- New unit tests: `ApollonWithConnection.test.tsx` (loading/error/collab-prompt regression), `WebSocketManager.test.ts`, `sharedDiagramStorage.test.ts`.

#### Trade-offs

- Thumbnail warmup renders an offscreen `ApollonEditor` per visible diagram on home load. Mitigated with idle scheduling and a cache; fine for typical usage but may want IndexedDB persistence for users with very many diagrams.
- Old single-segment shared links (`/:id?view=`) keep working only via the redirect shim; links without a `view` param now resolve to the error page.

### Steps for Testing

1. Open the app at `/`. The "Your diagrams" dashboard should render (not the editor) with the New diagram / Template / Import header actions.
2. With an empty store (clear localStorage `persistenceModelStore`), the gallery shows the "No diagrams yet" empty state with New diagram / From template / Import buttons.
3. Click **New diagram** → pick a type and name → you land on `/local/<uuid>` in the editor.
4. Edit the diagram, then return home — the card thumbnail should reflect your edits.
5. Open a card's three-dot menu → **Duplicate** (new card appears) and → **Delete** → confirm (card disappears).
6. Use search, the type/sort dropdowns, the **All/Local/Shared** segmented control, and the **Favorites** toggle; the count and list should update.
7. Switch between **grid** and **table** views.
8. Add 10+ diagrams and scroll the gallery — more cards load automatically (no "Load more" button).
9. Share a local diagram (card menu → Share), copy the link, then open `/shared/<id>?view=…` in a new tab.
10. Visit an old-style link `/<id>?view=EDIT` — it should redirect to `/shared/<id>?view=EDIT`.
11. Open an unavailable shared link — the editor should show the load-error screen with Retry / Go home.
12. Toggle the theme switcher; home colours should swap via the `--home-*` tokens.
13. Run `npx playwright test tests/e2e/home-page.spec.ts` — all 28 tests should pass.

### Screenshots

body:
### Checklist

- [x] I linked PR with a related issue  
- [ ] I added multiple screenshots/screencasts of my UI changes

### Motivation and Context

This PR completes https://github.com/ls1intum/Apollon/issues/583

Apollon previously relied on `currentModelId` in client state to determine the active local diagram while keeping the app on `/`. This caused missing shareable local URLs, weak browser back/forward behavior, and tab/session conflicts.  
In addition, `/:diagramId` for shared diagrams conflicted with the need to support local diagram IDs in the URL.

### Description

This PR introduces explicit URL-based routing and makes the URL the source of truth for active diagrams.

- Added dedicated routes:
1. `/` reserved as home placeholder (no editor mounting)
2. `/local/:id` for local persisted diagrams
3. `/shared/:id` for shared/server-backed diagrams
4. `/playground` unchanged

- Migrated local editor selection logic:
1. `ApollonLocal` now reads diagram ID from `useParams()`
2. Diagram is loaded from persistence store by URL ID
3. Removed auto-creation of default class diagram in `ApollonLocal`
4. Invalid/missing local ID renders `ErrorPage` with navigation back to `/`

- Migrated shared diagram route:
1. Replaced old `/:diagramId` usage with `/shared/:id`
2. Updated `ApollonWithConnection` parameter handling

- Updated all local diagram navigation entry points to URL-based navigation (`/local/${id}`):
1. `NewDiagramModal`
2. `NewDiagramFromTemplateModal`
3. `LoadDiagramModal`
4. JSON import flow in `NavbarFile` (`JsonFileImportButton`)

- Updated sharing flow:
1. Shared links now use `/shared/${id}?view=...`
2. In-app navigation after share now uses `/shared/:id`

- Removed active-local-diagram runtime dependency on `currentModelId` in local flow-related behavior (URL now drives local diagram selection).

### Steps for Testing

1. Start the webapp and open `/`
2. Verify home placeholder is shown and no local editor is mounted
3. Create a new diagram from `File -> New File`
4. Verify navigation to `/local/:id` and editor loads the created diagram
5. Create a diagram from template
6. Verify navigation to `/local/:id` and editor loads correctly
7. Import a JSON diagram from `File -> Import`
8. Verify navigation to `/local/:id` for imported model
9. Open `Load Diagram`, select another diagram
10. Verify URL changes to `/local/:id` and selected diagram loads
11. Open `/local/non-existing-id` manually
12. Verify `ErrorPage` is shown with a back-to-home action
13. Click `Share`, choose any view (Edit/Collaborate/Give Feedback/See Feedback)
14. Verify copied and navigated link format is `/shared/:id?view=...`
15. Open browser back/forward between local routes
16. Verify navigation restores corresponding local diagrams
17. Open two different `/local/:id` URLs in two tabs
18. Verify tabs operate independently

### Screenshots

### Checklist

- [ ] I linked PR with a related issue
- [ ] I added multiple screenshots/screencasts of my UI changes

### Motivation and Context

Assessment popovers stopped opening in writable assessment mode after quiz-mode interaction gating changes.  
The popup-open logic was coupled to `isDiagramModifiable`, which is intentionally false in assessment mode, so double-click did not open the assessment popup and Artemis modeling assessment flows broke.

This PR completes https://github.com/ls1intum/Apollon/issues/xx

### Description

This PR introduces a minimal, source-level fix in Apollon interaction handling:

- Updated `useElementInteractions` so popovers can open when:
1. diagram is modifiable (existing behavior), or
2. editor is in `ApollonMode.Assessment` and not readonly.

Concretely:
- Added `canOpenAssessmentPopover = mode === ApollonMode.Assessment && !readonly`
- Added `canOpenPopover = isDiagramModifiable || canOpenAssessmentPopover`
- Replaced double-click guards in `onNodeDoubleClick` and `onEdgeDoubleClick` to use `canOpenPopover`

What stays unchanged:
- Delete protection still uses `isDiagramModifiable` (`onBeforeDelete`)
- No broad refactor, no behavior changes outside popup opening conditions

### Steps for Testing

1. Build Apollon library:
   - `npm run build:lib`
2. Open a diagram in assessment mode with `readonly = false`.
3. Double-click a node:
   - Verify assessment popover opens (score/feedback fields visible).
4. Double-click an edge:
   - Verify edge assessment popover opens.
5. Switch to readonly assessment mode:
   - Verify give-feedback popovers are not opened by editing interactions.
6. Switch to modelling mode:
   - Verify existing modelling popup behavior remains unchanged.
7. Regression check with Artemis modeling assessment flow:
   - Run the previously failing modeling assessment e2e path and verify it no longer times out waiting for assessment fields.

body:
### Checklist

- [x] I linked PR with a related issue
- [x] I added multiple screenshots/screencasts of my UI changes

### Motivation and Context

This PR completes https://github.com/ls1intum/Apollon/issues/392

Edges between diagram elements were too rigid: straight connections did not expose useful drag handles, manually adjusted paths could be recalculated when connected nodes moved, and edge controls could overlap with drag handles. This made it difficult to create clean UML-style paths similar to draw.io or Lucidchart.

### Description

This PR improves edge handling and path editing by:

- adding drag handles for straight edge paths so users can reshape direct connections
- keeping edge paths orthogonal, without angled/tilted segments
- converting straight edges into editable orthogonal detours when dragged
- collapsing detoured edges back to a straight path when the user returns the dragger to the original alignment
- preserving custom edge routes when connected nodes move
- ensuring endpoint approach segments respect the side they are attached to, so arrows keep the correct direction
- preferring straight connections when drawing a new edge if the selected source/target sides allow it
- falling back to the regular bent route when a straight connection is not possible
- improving edge toolbar placement so it avoids overlapping edge draggers
- adding unit tests for edge utility behavior and toolbar positioning

### Steps for Testing

1. Create two nodes in a step-based diagram, e.g. Class, Component, Deployment, BPMN, or Flowchart.
2. Draw an edge between facing sides, e.g. right side of node A to left side of node B.
3. Verify that Apollon creates a straight edge when possible.
4. Select the straight edge and verify that a drag handle appears.
5. Drag the handle and verify that the edge becomes an orthogonal detour without diagonal/tilted segments.
6. Drag the handle back to the original straight alignment and verify that the edge collapses cleanly back to straight.
7. Move one of the connected nodes and verify that the custom route is preserved instead of being fully recalculated.
8. Connect nodes from sides that cannot form a straight edge and verify that Apollon still creates the regular bent edge instead of discarding the connection.
9. Select an edge with draggers and verify that the edit/delete toolbar does not sit directly on top of a dragger.

### Checklist

- [ ] I linked PR with a related issue

### Motivation and Context

This PR improves Apollon’s generic interactive element selection flow used by downstream integrations such as Artemis drag-and-drop quiz generation.

Previously, the Highlight/interactive selection flow only worked reliably for top-level nodes and relationships. Nested class diagram elements, such as attributes and methods, could not be selected for cutouts, stale selections could remain after changes, and selected relationships were visually hard to distinguish.

### Description

This PR extends the existing generic Highlight view instead of introducing an Artemis-specific quiz mode.

The changes include:
- Support selecting nested node elements, including class attributes and methods.
- Persist nested selections in `model.interactive.elements`.
- Prune stale interactive IDs when nodes, nested elements, or relationships are removed.
- Preserve explicit empty interactive selections so consumers can distinguish “nothing selected” from legacy diagrams without interactive data.
- Improve selected-element styling with a distinct interactive selection color.
- Keep the existing modeling mode as the default unless the Highlight view is explicitly enabled by the embedding application.

### Steps for Testing

1. Start the Apollon webapp.
2. Create a class diagram with at least one class containing attributes and methods.
3. Enable the Highlight/interactive selection view.
4. Select a class, an attribute, a method, and a relationship.
5. Verify selected elements are visibly highlighted with the interactive selection color.
6. Export or inspect the editor model and verify selected node/attribute/method IDs appear in `interactive.elements`, while selected relationships appear in `interactive.relationships`.
7. Deselect all elements and verify `interactive` is still serialized as empty records instead of falling back to stale selections.
8. Delete selected nodes or relationships and verify deleted IDs are removed from `interactive`.
9. Return to normal modeling view and verify regular modeling behavior is unchanged.

### Checklist

- [ ] I linked PR with a related issue
- [ ] I added multiple screenshots/screencasts of my UI changes

### Motivation and Context

The release workflows currently call `gh release create ... --verify-tag` for both the library and standalone releases, but they do not create the required git tags beforehand.

That means a release can partially succeed and then fail at GitHub Release creation when the expected tag is missing:
- library expects `@tumaet/apollon@<version>`
- standalone expects `v<version>`

This PR fixes that release-flow gap by making CI create the required tag automatically before running the verified GitHub release step.

### Description

This PR updates the release workflows so they no longer depend on a manually created tag.

Changes:
- `release-library.yml`
  - detects whether the GitHub release still needs to be created
  - creates `@tumaet/apollon@<version>` if it does not already exist
  - keeps `--verify-tag` in place for `gh release create`
  - handles the rerun case where npm publish already succeeded but the GitHub release/tag is still missing
- `release-standalone.yml`
  - creates `v<version>` if it does not already exist
  - keeps `--verify-tag` in place for `gh release create`

### Steps for Testing

1. Review `.github/workflows/release-library.yml` and confirm the workflow now ensures `@tumaet/apollon@<version>` exists before `gh release create --verify-tag`.
2. Review `.github/workflows/release-standalone.yml` and confirm the workflow now ensures `v<version>` exists before `gh release create --verify-tag`.
3. Confirm both workflows are idempotent when the tag already exists on `origin`.
4. Merge this PR before the next release bump PR and verify the next release no longer requires manual tag creation.

### Motivation and Context

This PR addresses the quiz-element export workflow needed by Artemis drag-and-drop quiz generation and makes quiz interactions explicitly opt-in.

### Description

This PR introduces an opt-in Quiz Mode flow in Apollon while keeping default behavior unchanged for existing consumers.

Main changes:
- Added an enableQuizMode option (default false) so quiz-related UI and interactions are only active when explicitly enabled.
- Added view handling for Modelling and Highlight states, used to switch between normal modelling and quiz-element selection.
- Updated sidebar behavior so quiz controls are hidden unless quiz mode is enabled.
- Added interactive element/relationship tracking in diagram state and model serialization (interactive field in model).
- Added utility functions to toggle and prune interactive selections.
- Integrated export filtering for include/exclude use cases and switched export bounds calculation to rendered DOM-based bounds merging (nodes, edges, overflow, text).
- Added Playground control (Enable Quiz Mode checkbox) to test the feature without exposing quiz controls by default in normal usage.

Compatibility:
- Existing integrations remain unaffected unless enableQuizMode is set to true.
- Default editor behavior remains normal modelling mode with no quiz-specific controls.

### Steps for Testing

1. Build and test:
1. Run npm run build:lib
2. Run npm run build:webapp
3. Run npm run test --workspace=@tumaet/apollon

2. Playground behavior:
1. Run npm run dev and open /playground
2. Verify Enable Quiz Mode is unchecked by default
3. Verify sidebar renders normal modelling tools only
4. Enable Quiz Mode and verify Model / Quiz Elements controls appear
5. Switch to Quiz Elements and click nodes/edges to mark them

Use this PR body:

### Checklist

- [x] I linked PR with a related issue
- [ ] I added multiple screenshots/screencasts of my UI changes

### Motivation and Context

This PR fixes inconsistent required-interface rendering in component and deployment diagrams and resolves incorrect interface sizing when importing legacy diagrams.

Fixes #565

### Description

This PR makes required interfaces render more consistently and keeps imported interface nodes aligned with the current geometry rules.

Changes included:
- normalize imported legacy component/deployment interface nodes to the standard interface size while preserving their center position
- centralize shared interface geometry used by markers, edge padding, palette defaults, and flat SVG export
- make required-interface rendering follow consistent rules in both component and deployment diagrams:
  - a single required edge keeps the normal required socket
  - a provided edge on the same interface no longer shrinks that single required socket
  - multiple required edges on the same interface use a reduced variant only when needed
  - opposite-side required edges keep the normal variant
- extract the required-interface variant selection into a shared utility used by both diagram types
- fix the Windows dev launcher so `.cmd`/`.bat` processes are spawned correctly without `spawn EINVAL`

### Steps for Testing

1. Run `npm run build:lib` and confirm the library builds successfully.
2. On Windows, run `npm run dev` and verify the dev launcher no longer fails with `spawn EINVAL`.
3. Import a legacy v3 component or deployment diagram containing interface nodes with `20x20` bounds and verify the interfaces are normalized to the current standard size.
4. In a component diagram, connect one required edge to an interface and verify the socket renders with the normal single-edge appearance.
5. Add a provided edge to the same interface and verify the single required socket does not become smaller.
6. Add multiple required edges to the same interface and verify reduced variants are only used where needed, while opposite-side connections keep the normal variant.
7. Export a diagram with required interfaces as flat SVG and verify the exported socket geometry matches the editor rendering.

Closes #566 ### Description The exported SVG clip bounds were computed in flow/global coordinates, but label text bounds were partially measured in local nested SVG coordinates. This caused labels to be cut off in some exports. Changes in this PR: - Updated text bounds calculation in `library/lib/utils/exportUtils.ts`. - For node labels: convert local `getBBox()` text bounds to flow coordinates using node transform and node SVG `viewBox` scaling. - For edge labels: measure rendered text with `getBoundingClientRect()` and map to flow coordinates via `screenToFlowPosition(...)`. - Merge text bounds into final export bounds before generating the clip rectangle. - Removed temporary debugging logs after verification. ### Steps for Testing 1. Run the app in dev mode: - `npm run dev` 2. Open the standalone webapp. 3.
